sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, presents with anxiety regarding recent blood work showing hepatitis C, which was not previously known to her.
She reports feeling fine despite this diagnosis.
She mentions a history of IV drug use in the past, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She also reports a history of excessive alcohol use, currently consuming about 1 beer a day, and smoking 1-2 cigarettes a day, down from a previous pack and a half a day.
She mentions feeling tired at the end of the day, but attributes this to work-related stress.
Her family history includes hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Past medical history is otherwise unremarkable.
Review of systems: Fatigue.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
Heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
Lung exam is clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Abdomen is soft with present bowel sounds and no hepatosplenomegaly.
Musculoskeletal exam is unremarkable with no gait disturbance or edema.
Test Results: HCV antibody test is positive, liver panel shows elevated AST at 39, but ALT and total bilirubin are within normal limits.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, which is a viral infection that affects the liver, likely acquired years ago.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; Suspected liver disease; Suspected liver fibrosis.
Plan:
I prescribed hepatitis C RNA testing and HCV genotype testing to confirm the diagnosis and determine the severity of liver disease.
I also ordered an ultrasound to assess liver fibrosis.
I advised the patient to schedule hepatitis C screening for her family members, starting with her children.
I instructed the nurse to schedule follow-up appointments for the patient in three weeks.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three weeks for follow-up and further management of her hepatitis C.
